special_mechanics.name: Point-and-Click Navigation
special_mechanics.description: Game uses point-and-click interface with 15 action commands (Walk To, Unlock, etc.) rather than real-time platformer movement

special_mechanics.name: Character-Specific Abilities
special_mechanics.description: Each of six playable characters (Dave, Bernard, Jeff, Michael, Razor, Syd, Wendy) possesses unique puzzle-solving abilities affecting gameplay paths

tier_notes: Tier 2 empirical/qualitative data. Maniac Mansion NES is a graphic adventure game, not a platformer. Movement parameters are not mechanically relevant as gameplay relies on point-and-click interface and puzzle-solving rather than physics-based character control. No jump physics, gravity, or sub-pixel system documentation exists for this title.
sources_used: Wikipedia - Maniac Mansion (1987 game overview and development)
